task_completion.description: Create a new mask for segmentation
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a newly created mask in the 'Masks' panel/list in the InVesalius GUI, distinct from any default masks.

task_completion.description: Navigate to the specified Axial slices
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the Axial view (usually top-left) and verify the slice slider or text indicator shows slice 60, 61, or 62.

task_completion.description: Manually segment a circular target region in the center of the brain
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a colored overlay (matching the mask color) painted in a roughly circular shape in the center of the brain tissue on the Axial view.

task_completion.description: Generate a 3D surface from the segmentation
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a 3D object/surface appearing in the 3D viewer panel (usually bottom-right), which corresponds to the shape of the painted mask.

task_completion.description: Export the 3D surface as an STL file to the correct path
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for an 'Export 3D surface' dialog or a file manager window showing the file being saved as 'target_volume.stl' in the '/home/ga/Documents/' directory.

integrity.description: Agent used the InVesalius GUI to perform the task
integrity.visual_evidence: Check that the agent is clicking through the InVesalius 3 interface (using brush tools, slice sliders, and export menus) rather than running terminal scripts to bypass the GUI.

integrity.description: The 3D surface was genuinely generated from the manual mask
integrity.visual_evidence: Ensure the 3D surface visible in the viewer visually corresponds to the manual brush strokes seen on the 2D axial slices, proving it wasn't a pre-existing STL loaded into the viewer.
Verify the active window is InVesalius 3 and not a generic 3D viewer, text editor, or web browser.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The expected output file path is /home/ga/Documents/target_volume.stl. The target slices for manual segmentation are Axial Slices 60, 61, and 62. The DICOM series used is ct_cranium/0051.
